questions.context:
[Situation: acknowledging a schedule conflict when a room is unavailable]
Speaker: "Where are we supposed to meet if the room's booked?"
questions.stem: Where are we supposed to meet if the room's booked?

options.A: Actually, the lounge is free downstairs.
options.B: The room has a brand-new projector.
options.C: She finished her essay last night.

questions.context:
[Situation: a student asking a librarian why a resource is missing]
Speaker: "Why isn't this journal showing up in the catalog?"
questions.stem: Why isn't this journal showing up in the catalog?

options.A: The catalog was redesigned last spring.
options.B: Well, it might still be processing.
options.C: I returned my books this morning.

questions.context:
[Situation: expressing concern about a friend who had a rough day]
Speaker: "Are you sure you're okay after yesterday?"
questions.stem: Are you sure you're okay after yesterday?

options.B: The bus was late this morning.
options.C: Don't worry, I'm back on my feet.

questions.context:
[Situation: asking a passerby for directions to a nearby station]
Speaker: "What's the fastest way to the train station?"
questions.stem: What's the fastest way to the train station?

options.A: The station added a new coffee stand.
options.B: I love taking long train rides.
options.D: Your best bet is down Elm Street.

questions.context:
[Situation: two partners deciding how to divide a presentation]
Speaker: "How should we split up the presentation slides?"
questions.stem: How should we split up the presentation slides?

options.A: I'm happy to take the lead.
options.B: The slides have a nice blue theme.
options.C: My laptop battery died last night.

questions.context:
[Situation: checking whether the post office is still open]
Speaker: "I need to mail this package before five."
questions.stem: I need to mail this package before five.

options.B: Actually, the post office closes at four today.
options.C: I already paid the electric bill.
options.D: No, I haven't seen your keys.

questions.context:
[Situation: a friend worrying about getting to the airport]
Speaker: "I'm not sure how I'll get to the airport."
questions.stem: I'm not sure how I'll get to the airport.

options.A: The airport parking is really expensive.
options.B: I finally finished packing last night.
options.C: Don't sweat it, I'll drive you.
options.D: Yes, the flight was pretty smooth.

questions.context:
[Situation: responding to a dinner invitation]
Speaker: "Do you want to join us for dinner?"
questions.stem: Do you want to join us for dinner?

options.A: Dinner was delicious at that place.
options.C: I'll join the gym next week.
options.D: Count me in, that sounds great.

questions.context:
[Situation: two classmates confirming a project deadline]
Speaker: "When is the group project actually due?"
questions.stem: When is the group project actually due?

options.A: The project needs three more sections.
options.B: Didn't the professor push it to Monday?
options.C: I left my notebook in the lab.

questions.context:
[Situation: reacting when a grocery bag is about to break]
Speaker: "This grocery bag is about to rip."
questions.stem: This grocery bag is about to rip.

options.A: Here, let me grab that side.
options.B: The grocery store restocked this week.
options.C: I watered the plants this morning.
options.D: No, the receipt's in my pocket.
